rulings.Case Title: WALTER YEE et al VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday September 9, 2016, line 1. DEFENDANT THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiffs characterize their complaint as "clumsy" and suggest that the gravamen of the complaint is based on "City's inability to deliver on its promised solution for the shortfall in square footage or its decision not to deliver on its promise" (Opposition 3:7-8).The complaint, as currently alleged, does not state any causes of action based on such "promise." Plaintiff shall have 10 days to file an amended complaint as suggested in the opposition (see also Yee Declaration, 7:20-22 and 8:9-14) to state cause(s) of action that are not barred by the statute of limitations, have to been released by the settlement agreement, are not required to be addressed by a writ proceeding, and for which exhaustion of administrative remedies is not necessary. = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL DOUGLAS ET AL VS. DAHLIA HOTEL, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel For Plaintiff Stevie Jackson
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday September 9, 2016, line 2. PLAINTIFF MICHAEL DOUGLAS Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel For Plaintiff Stevie Jackson IS GRANTED. No opposition filed. Counsel to prepare order. =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: LYDIA JENKINS VS. MARIA PALACPAC-LUGA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday September 9, 2016, line 3. PLAINTIFF LYDIA JENKINS Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery IS OFF CALENDAR. Moving party failed to obtain relief under CCP 2024.050 before bringing this motion within 15 days of trial. (Moving party only obtained an Order Shortening Time). =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREW KANE ET AL VS. LSL PROPERTIES, B14 DE, LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday September 9, 2016, line 4. DEFENDANT LSL PROPERTIES, B14 DE, LLC DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is overruled as to the causes of action for SFRO and UCL violation. These causes of action are sufficiently alleged as to the named Plaintiffs. Demurrer to the cause of action for declaratory relief is sustained without leave to amend as plaintiffs have an adequate remedy at law. Demurrer to class action allegations is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege a viable class claim in light of the issues raised by the demurrer (including but not limited to numerosity and commonality) and the relief sought in the properly alleged causes of action. (Compare putative class paragraph 88 and paragraphs 103, 115). =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: TIMOTHY KIM VS. LESLIE'S BEAUTY SALON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion Granting Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday September 9, 2016, line 7. PLAINTIFF TIMOTHY KIM Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint is OFF CALENDAR, insufficient notice per CCP 1005, 1013. =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: ELIZABETH GOMEZ VS. ERLINDA SELMA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday September 9, 2016, line 5. PLAINTIFF MARIA LABITORIA Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs is granted in part: the amount of $800.53 is taxed; Defendant to recover $2,871.47 incurred by the non-profit organization in defending the defendant/prevailing party. (See Ex. A and B to Hill Declaration). =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: ELIZABETH GULI ET AL VS. ALBERTO LOPEZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday September 9, 2016, line 6. DEFENDANT ALBERTO LOPEZ, ANA SOLORZON, RUDY MAGANA, ROCIO QUIJANO DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. (a) Unlawful detainer actions, including actions based on Ellis Act, require strict compliance. Ellis Act does not provide any explicit exception to the general long-standing unlawful detainer requirements. See Lamey v. Masciorata (1969) 273 Cal.App.2d 709Legislature is charged with knowledge of such law/judicial decisions. People v. Overstreet (1986) 42 Cal.3d 891. (b) Defendant failed to provide any authority for its proposed reading of SFRO 37.9A(E). This provision does not explicitly require that only "one" notice shall be given ("shall give written notice") with all the information required by subsections (i)-(iii) ; it also does not explicitly bar multiple notices or filing of amended, additional, supplemental or clarifying notices within 90 days of the notice of intent to withdraw . (c) Parties' requests for judicial notice are granted. Evid. Code 452(h). =(501/AJR)
